title: Zork I: The Great Underground Empire
author: Marc Blanc, Dave Lebling, et al. (Infocom, 1980)
description: The original underground adventure. Source code released under MIT by Microsoft (Nov 2025). This is an LLM-narrated port — the engine is ours, the world is Infocom's.
intro:
ZORK I: The Great Underground Empire
Infocom interactive fiction — a fantasy story.
Copyright (c) 1981 Infocom, Inc. Source code released under MIT license, Microsoft 2025.
systemPromptOverride:
This is Zork I, the original 1980 Infocom underground adventure. Narrate in the spirit of the canonical Infocom prose:

Voice
- Terse, dry, slightly archaic. Direct without flourish.
- Atmospheric for dark caves, ancient ruins, and moments of dread; matter-of-fact for routine actions.
- Occasionally wry, never breaking the fourth wall.

Style
- Short concrete sentences. Two or three is usually enough; a single sentence is often better.
- Second person, present tense ("You see…"). Never "I" or "we".
- Plain language. No modern slang, no emoji, no exclamation marks except for genuine peril or surprise.
- The player is "you," sometimes "the adventurer."

Echoes of the original
- Don't paraphrase canonical text — when the engine returns the canonical room or item description, prefer it as-is unless the situation demands flourish.
- The world is the Great Underground Empire. Treasures, trolls, thieves, and grues live here. If the player wanders into total darkness without a light source, channel the famous warning: it is pitch black; you are likely to be eaten by a grue.

Discipline
- Never invent items, rooms, exits, or events the engine hasn't surfaced.
- If the engine rejects an action, refuse in character — don't pretend it succeeded.
- Treat trademarked references gently: this port runs on the MIT-licensed source; the "ZORK" brand isn't ours, so don't lean on the trademark in narration.

NPC interaction (story-specific tools)
- This story exposes NPC verb tools like `talk-to-troll`, `talk-to-cyclops`, `cyclops-magic-word`, plus the generic `give(itemId, targetId)` for handing items to NPCs. When the player engages an NPC conversationally ("insult the troll", "shout at the cyclops", "bargain with the thief"), call the matching tool BEFORE narrating the dialogue. The tool call lets author triggers fire (aggravation, befriending, mood shifts). Then narrate the NPC's response in character using its `personality` field.
- Examples of player phrasing → tool: "kill troll" → `attack(troll, sword)` if armed; "say ulysses" / "shout odysseus" at the cyclops → `cyclops-magic-word()`; "give the lunch to the cyclops" → `give(itemId="lunch", targetId="cyclops")` (one item per call); "give the egg to the thief" → `give(itemId="egg", targetId="thief")`.

Score
- The view includes a `score` field (current / max / moves / rank). Don't volunteer it unless the player asks ("score", "how am I doing?", etc.) — when they do, call the `score` tool. The engine emits the canonical "Your score is N (total of M), in K moves. This gives you the rank of RANK." sentence; pass it through verbatim.
- When narration cues mention "+N points" or "-N points", weave them naturally into your prose — they're already authored.

rooms.name: West of House
rooms.description: You are standing in an open field west of a white house, with a boarded front door.

southwest.blockedMessage: The path to the barrow lies in shadow. It will not open to you yet.

in.blockedMessage: The path to the barrow lies in shadow. It will not open to you yet.

rooms.name: Stone Barrow
rooms.description: You are standing in front of a massive barrow of stone. In the east face is a huge stone door which is open. You cannot see into the dark of the tomb.

rooms.name: North of House
rooms.description: You are facing the north side of a white house. There is no door here, and all the windows are boarded up. To the north a narrow path winds through the trees.

rooms.name: South of House
rooms.description: You are facing the south side of a white house. There is no door here, and all the windows are boarded.

rooms.name: Behind House
rooms.description: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window.

variants.text: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is open.
You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a forest, with trees in all directions. To the east, there appears to be sunlight.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a dimly lit forest, with large trees all around.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: The forest thins out, revealing impassable mountains.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a dimly lit forest, with large trees all around.

rooms.name: Forest Path
rooms.description: This is a path winding through a dimly lit forest. The path heads north-south here. One particularly large tree with some low branches stands at the edge of the path.

rooms.name: Up a Tree
rooms.description: You are about 10 feet above the ground nestled among some large branches. The nearest branch above you is above your reach.

rooms.name: Clearing
rooms.description: You are in a small clearing in a well marked forest path that extends to the east and west.

rooms.name: Clearing
rooms.description: You are in a small clearing in a well marked forest path that extends to the east and west.

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.description: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window.

variants.text: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is open.

You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.name: Attic
rooms.description: This is the attic. The only exit is a stairway leading down.

rooms.name: Living Room
rooms.description: You are in the living room. To the east, a doorway opens into the kitchen. A trophy case stands against one wall.

rooms.name: Cellar
rooms.description: You are in a dark and damp cellar with a narrow passageway leading north, and a crawlway to the south. On the west is the bottom of a steep metal ramp which is unclimbable.

rooms.name: The Troll Room
rooms.description: This is a small room with passages to the east and south and a forbidding hole leading west. Bloodstains and deep scratches (perhaps made by an axe) mar the walls.

east.blockedMessage: The troll, brandishing his bloody axe, blocks your way.

west.blockedMessage: The troll, brandishing his bloody axe, blocks your way.

variants.text: This is a small room with passages to the east and south and a forbidding hole leading west. Bloodstains and deep scratches mar the walls. The troll is gone — only signs of the fight remain.

rooms.name: East of Chasm
rooms.description: You are on the east edge of a chasm, the bottom of which cannot be seen. A narrow passage goes north, and the path you are on continues to the east.

rooms.name: Gallery
rooms.description: This is an art gallery. Most of the paintings have been stolen by vandals with exceptional taste. The vandals left through either the north or west exits.

rooms.name: Studio
rooms.description: This appears to have been an artist's studio. The walls and floors are splattered with paints of 69 different colors. Strangely enough, nothing of value is hanging here. At the south end of the room is an open door (also covered with paint). A dark and narrow chimney leads up from a fireplace; although you might be able to get up it, it seems unlikely you could get back down.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike. A skeleton, probably the remains of a luckless adventurer, lies here.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Grating Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room near the maze. There are twisty passages in the immediate vicinity. Above you is a grating with a skull-and-crossbones lock.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Cyclops Room
rooms.description: This room has an exit on the northwest, and a staircase leading up.

up.blockedMessage: The cyclops watches the staircase with his single fierce eye — you'd never get past him alive.

variants.text: This room has an exit on the northwest, a staircase leading up, and a ragged cyclops-shaped hole smashed clean through the east wall.

rooms.name: Strange Passage
rooms.description: This is a long passage. To the west is one entrance. On the east there is an old wooden door, with a large opening in it (about cyclops sized).

rooms.name: Treasure Room
rooms.description: This is a large room, whose east wall is solid granite. A number of discarded bags, which crumble at your touch, are scattered about on the floor. There is an exit down a staircase.

rooms.name: Reservoir South
rooms.description: You are in a long room on the south shore of a large lake, far too deep and wide for crossing. There is a path along the stream to the east or west, a steep pathway climbing southwest along the edge of a chasm, and a path leading into a canyon to the southeast.

north.blockedMessage: The reservoir is too deep and the water far too cold — you'd drown before reaching the other side.

variants.text: You are at the south end of what was once a large lake. With the reservoir drained, the lake bed lies exposed — a stretch of dark mud, silt, and the occasional rusted relic. A path runs east and west; a steep pathway climbs southwest along the edge of a chasm; a canyon opens to the southeast. To the north, the dry bed leads into the reservoir proper.

rooms.name: Reservoir
rooms.description: You are on what used to be a large lake, but which is now a large mud pile. There are "shores" to the north and south.

variants.text:
You are on what used to be a large lake, but which is now a large mud pile. There are "shores" to the north and south.

Lying half buried in the mud is an old trunk, bulging with jewels.

rooms.name: Reservoir North
rooms.description: You are in a large cavernous room, the south of which is a wide, deep stream — the head of the reservoir. There is a slimy stairway leaving the room to the north.

variants.text: You are in a large cavernous room. The southern half, formerly a wide, deep stream feeding the reservoir, is now reduced to a sluggish trickle through mud. A slimy stairway leaves the room to the north.

rooms.name: Stream View
rooms.description: You are standing on a path beside a gently flowing stream. The path follows the stream, which flows from west to east.

rooms.name: Stream
rooms.description: You are on the gently flowing stream. The upstream route is too narrow to navigate, and the downstream route is invisible due to twisting walls. There is a narrow beach to land on.

rooms.name: Mirror Room
rooms.description: You are in a large square room with tall ceilings. On the south wall is an enormous mirror which fills the entire wall. There are exits on the other three sides of the room.

rooms.name: Mirror Room
rooms.description: You are in a large square room with tall ceilings. On the south wall is an enormous mirror which fills the entire wall. There are exits on the other three sides of the room.

rooms.name: Cave
rooms.description: This is a tiny cave with entrances west and north, and a staircase leading down.

rooms.name: Cave
rooms.description: This is a tiny cave with entrances west and north, and a dark, forbidding staircase leading down.

rooms.name: Cold Passage
rooms.description: This is a cold and damp corridor where a long east-west passageway turns into a southward path.

rooms.name: Narrow Passage
rooms.description: This is a long and narrow corridor where a long north-south passageway briefly narrows even further.

rooms.name: Winding Passage
rooms.description: This is a winding passage. It seems that there are only exits on the east and north.

rooms.name: Twisting Passage
rooms.description: This is a winding passage. It seems that there are only exits on the east and north.

rooms.name: Atlantis Room
rooms.description: This is an ancient room, long under water. There is an exit to the south and a staircase leading up.

rooms.name: East-West Passage
rooms.description: This is a narrow east-west passageway. There is a narrow stairway leading down at the north end of the room.

rooms.name: Round Room
rooms.description: This is a circular stone room with passages in all directions. Several of them have unfortunately been blocked by cave-ins.

rooms.name: Deep Canyon
rooms.description: You are on the south edge of a deep canyon. Passages lead off to the east, northwest and southwest. A stairway leads down. You can hear a loud roaring sound, like that of rushing water, from below.

rooms.name: Damp Cave
rooms.description: This cave has exits to the west and east, and narrows to a crack toward the south. The earth is particularly damp here.

rooms.name: Loud Room
rooms.description: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is deafeningly loud with an undetermined rushing sound. The sound seems to be reverberating from all of the walls, making it difficult even to think.

variants.text: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is unnaturally quiet now, the rushing sound silenced as if it had never been.

This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is unbearably loud — an ear-splitting torrent of water roars from somewhere unseen, drowning every other sense.

rooms.name: North-South Passage
rooms.description: This is a high north-south passage, which forks to the northeast.

rooms.name: Chasm
rooms.description: A chasm runs southwest to northeast and the path follows it. You are on the south side of the chasm, where a crack opens into a passage.

rooms.name: Entrance to Hades
rooms.description: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| The gate is open; through it you can see a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. Thousands of voices, lamenting some hideous fate, can be heard. The way through the gate is barred by evil spirits, who jeer at your attempts to pass.

in.blockedMessage: The evil spirits jeer and bar your passage — you cannot enter the Land of the Dead until they have been driven off.

south.blockedMessage: The evil spirits jeer and bar your passage — you cannot enter the Land of the Dead until they have been driven off.

variants.text: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| The gate is open; through it you can see a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The wailing voices have fallen silent — the spirits that barred your passage have fled. The way is clear.

You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| Beyond the gate, a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The bell lies on the ground where it fell, the red glow gone out of it. The candles burn with strange dancing flames. The spirits cower, trembling.

You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| Beyond the gate, a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. The bell lies on the ground where it fell, the red glow gone out of it. The spirits stand paralyzed, watching you with the expression of long-forgotten terror.

rooms.name: Land of the Dead
rooms.description: You have entered the Land of the Living Dead. Thousands of lost souls can be heard weeping and moaning. In the corner are stacked the remains of dozens of previous adventurers less fortunate than yourself. A passage exits to the north.

rooms.name: Engravings Cave
rooms.description: You have entered a low cave with passages leading northwest and east.

rooms.name: Egyptian Room
rooms.description: This is a room which looks like an Egyptian tomb. There is an ascending staircase to the west.

rooms.name: Dome Room
rooms.description: You are at the periphery of a large dome, which forms the ceiling of another room below. Protecting you from a precipitous drop is a wooden railing which circles the dome.

rooms.name: Torch Room
rooms.description: This is a large room with a prominent doorway leading to a down staircase. Above you is a large dome. Up around the edge of the dome (20 feet up) is a wooden railing. In the center of the room sits a white marble pedestal.

up.blockedMessage: The rope dangles overhead, but it ends some five feet above your head — too high to reach.

variants.text: This is a large room with a prominent doorway leading to a down staircase. Above you is a large dome, and a piece of rope descends from a wooden railing 20 feet up — the rope you tied, ending some five feet above your head. In the center of the room sits a white marble pedestal.

rooms.name: Temple
rooms.description: This is the north end of a large temple. On the east wall is an ancient inscription, probably a prayer in a long-forgotten language. Below the prayer is a staircase leading down. The west wall is solid granite. The exit to the north end of the room is through huge marble pillars.

rooms.name: Altar
rooms.description: This is the south end of a large temple. In front of you is what appears to be an altar. In one corner is a small hole in the floor which leads into darkness. You probably could not get back up it.

rooms.name: Dam
rooms.description: You are standing on the top of the Flood Control Dam #3, which was quite a tourist attraction in times far distant. There are paths to the north, south, and west, and a scramble down. A heavy iron bolt is mounted on the dam's weathered stonework, with a small green plastic bubble set directly above it.

rooms.name: Dam Lobby
rooms.description: This room appears to have been the waiting room for groups touring the dam. There are open doorways here to the north and east marked "Private", and there is a path leading south over the top of the dam.

rooms.name: Maintenance Room
rooms.description: This is what appears to have been the maintenance room for Flood Control Dam #3. Apparently, this room has been ransacked recently, for most of the valuable equipment is gone. On the wall in front of you is a group of buttons colored blue, yellow, brown, and red. There are doorways to the west and south.

rooms.name: Dam Base
rooms.description: You are at the base of Flood Control Dam #3, which looms above you and to the north. The river Frigid is flowing by here. Along the river are the White Cliffs which seem to form giant walls stretching from north to south along the shores of the river as it winds its way downstream.

north.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

up.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: You are on the Frigid River in the vicinity of the Dam. The river flows quietly here. There is a landing on the west shore.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river turns a corner here making it impossible to see the Dam. The White Cliffs loom on the east bank and large rocks prevent landing on the west.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river descends here into a valley. There is a narrow beach on the west shore below the cliffs. In the distance a faint rumbling can be heard.

rooms.name: White Cliffs Beach
rooms.description: You are on a narrow strip of beach which runs along the base of the White Cliffs. There is a narrow path heading south along the Cliffs and a tight passage leading west into the cliffs themselves.

south.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

west.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

rooms.name: White Cliffs Beach
rooms.description: You are on a rocky, narrow strip of beach beside the Cliffs. A narrow path leads north along the shore.

north.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river is running faster here and the sound ahead appears to be that of rushing water. On the east shore is a sandy beach. A small area of beach can also be seen below the cliffs on the west shore.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The sound of rushing water is nearly unbearable here. On the east shore is a large landing area.

rooms.name: Shore
rooms.description: You are on the east shore of the river. The water here seems somewhat treacherous. A path travels from north to south here, the south end quickly turning around a sharp corner.

north.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

south.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

rooms.name: Sandy Beach
rooms.description: You are on a large sandy beach on the east shore of the river, which is flowing quickly by. A path runs beside the river to the south here, and a passage is partially buried in sand to the northeast.

northeast.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

south.blockedMessage: The boat does not float on land.

rooms.name: Sandy Cave
rooms.description: This is a sand-filled cave whose exit is to the southwest.

rooms.name: Aragain Falls
rooms.description: You are at the top of Aragain Falls, an enormous waterfall with a drop of about 450 feet. The only path here is on the north end. A solid rainbow spans the falls.

rooms.name: On the Rainbow
rooms.description: You are on top of a rainbow (I bet you never thought you would walk on a rainbow), with a magnificent view of the Falls. The rainbow travels east-west here.

rooms.name: End of Rainbow
rooms.description: You are on a small, rocky beach on the continuation of the Frigid River past the Falls. The beach is narrow due to the presence of the White Cliffs. The river canyon opens here and sunlight shines in from above. A rainbow crosses over the falls to the east and a narrow path continues to the southwest.

rooms.name: Canyon Bottom
rooms.description: You are beneath the walls of the river canyon which may be climbable here. The lesser part of the runoff of Aragain Falls flows by below. To the north is a narrow path.

rooms.name: Rocky Ledge
rooms.description: You are on a ledge about halfway up the wall of the river canyon. You can see from here that the main flow from Aragain Falls twists along a passage which it is impossible for you to enter. Below you is the canyon bottom. Above you is more cliff, which appears climbable.

rooms.name: Canyon View
rooms.description: You are at the top of the Great Canyon on its west wall. From here there is a marvelous view of the canyon and parts of the Frigid River upstream. Across the canyon, the walls of the White Cliffs join the mighty ramparts of the Flathead Mountains to the east. Following the Canyon upstream to the north, Aragain Falls may be seen, complete with rainbow. The mighty Frigid River flows out from a great dark cavern. To the west and south can be seen an immense forest, stretching for miles around. A path leads northwest. It is possible to climb down into the canyon from here.

rooms.name: Mine Entrance
rooms.description: You are standing at the entrance of what might have been a coal mine. The shaft enters the west wall, and there is another exit on the south end of the room.

rooms.name: Squeaky Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room. Strange squeaky sounds may be heard coming from the passage at the north end. You may also escape to the east.

rooms.name: Bat Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room which has doors only to the east and south. In the corner of the room on the ceiling is a large vampire bat who is obviously deranged and holding his nose.

rooms.name: Shaft Room
rooms.description: This is a large room, in the middle of which is a small shaft descending through the floor into darkness below. To the west and the north are exits from this room. Constructed over the top of the shaft is a metal framework to which a heavy iron chain is attached.

rooms.name: Smelly Room
rooms.description: This is a small nondescript room. However, from the direction of a small descending staircase a foul odor can be detected. To the south is a narrow tunnel.

rooms.name: Gas Room
rooms.description: This is a small room which smells strongly of coal gas. There is a short climb up some stairs and a narrow tunnel leading east.

rooms.name: Ladder Top
rooms.description: This is a very small room. In the corner is a rickety wooden ladder, leading downward. It might be safe to descend. There is also a staircase leading upward.

rooms.name: Ladder Bottom
rooms.description: This is a rather wide room. On one side is the bottom of a narrow wooden ladder. To the west and the south are passages leaving the room.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the mine.

rooms.name: Timber Room
rooms.description: This is a long and narrow passage, which is cluttered with broken timbers. A wide passage comes from the east and turns at the west end of the room into a very narrow passageway. From the west comes a strong draft.

rooms.name: Drafty Room
rooms.description: This is a small drafty room in which is the bottom of a long shaft. To the south is a passageway and to the east a very narrow passage. In the shaft can be seen a heavy iron chain.

rooms.name: Machine Room
rooms.description: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid.

variants.text: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is open.

The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is closed.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Slide Room
rooms.description: This is a small chamber, which appears to have been part of a coal mine. On the south wall of the chamber the letters "Granite Wall" are etched in the rock. To the east is a long passage, and there is a steep metal slide twisting downward. To the north is a small opening.

items.name: board
items.description: The boards are securely fastened.

items.name: songbird
items.description: A small bright-feathered songbird, watching you with curious black eyes. Its trill is impossibly pure.
items.personality: A bright forest songbird with a clear, perfect trill. Doesn't speak — communicates through song. Curious about shiny things, especially the jewel-encrusted egg. Gentle, never threatening.

items.name: white house
items.description: The house is a beautiful colonial house which is painted white. It is clear that the owners must have been extremely wealthy.

items.name: forest
items.description: Dense pine and hemlock, the canopy filtering daylight to a green twilight.

items.name: tree
items.description: A large leaf-bearing tree, lower branches at climbing height.

items.name: mountain range
items.description: An impassable wall of high peaks — the Flatheads, named (legend has it) for their first king.

items.name: water
items.description: Cold dark water — drinkable, you suppose, though the thought isn't appetizing.

items.name: quantity of water
items.description: A measure of clear water, transparent and quite ordinary.
items.takeBlockedMessage: The water slips through your fingers.

items.name: number of ghosts
items.description: Translucent figures drifting through the air, faces blank with ancient grief. They take no notice of you.

items.name: crystal skull
items.description: A beautifully carved crystal skull, grinning at you rather nastily. A treasure.

items.name: lunch
items.description: A hot pepper sandwich wrapped tightly in waxed paper. Smells fierce.

items.name: bat
items.description: A sleek vampire bat, watching with bright unblinking eyes — and a mouth full of small sharp teeth.

items.name: brass bell
items.description: A small brass bell with a worn handle, polished smooth by use.

items.name: bloody axe
items.description: A heavy iron axe, its head crusted with dried blood.

items.name: bolt
items.description: A heavy iron bolt with a slot evidently sized for a wrench.
items.appearance: A heavy iron bolt protrudes from the dam's stonework.

items.personality: A suave, mustachioed gentleman thief in a black cloak. Speaks in clipped, ironic phrases ('How droll.', 'A trifle, surely?', 'A pleasure, as always.'). Connoisseur of theft — handles valuables with theatrical reverence, examines them with a critical eye before pocketing. Never crude, always composed. You will notice on your right that....

items.name: troll

items.appearance: A nasty-looking troll, hulking and brutish, brandishes a bloody axe.
items.personality: A nasty, hulking creature with greasy black hair and broken yellow tusks. Speaks in broken Common — short sentences, aggressive, third person ('Troll smash!'). Hates elves and dwarves; covets shiny things. If addressed in a friendly tone, suspicious. If threatened, roars. If knocked unconscious, snores and mutters about gold. items.description: An old wooden trunk, mud-caked and bulging with jewels. A treasure.

items.description: A lump of viscous greyish material, evidently good for sealing a puncture or two.

items.name: wall with engravings
items.description: Old engravings worked deep into stone — ancient writing, half worn away.

Skillfully interwoven with the bas reliefs are excerpts illustrating the major religious tenets of that time. Unfortunately, a later age seems to have considered them blasphemous and just as skillfully excised them.

items.name: ZORK owner's manual

If used and maintained in accordance with normal operating practices for small universes, ZORK will provide many months of trouble-free operation.

items.description: (control panel)

The egg appears extremely fragile.

The jewel-encrusted egg has been opened cleanly along its delicate clasp. The golden clockwork canary nestled inside is plainly visible, ready to be taken.
The jewel-encrusted egg has been opened cleanly along its delicate clasp, revealing the hollow within.

variants.text: The golden clockwork canary lies still — mechanism crushed, ruby eyes dulled, silver beak bent out of true, mainspring snapped.

items.name: stairs

A large wicker basket rests at your feet, the chain rising into the shaft above.
The chain dangles down into the shaft. The basket has been lowered out of sight below.

items.narratorNote: The basket is a freight elevator on a chain. It can only be moved by 'lower basket' or 'raise basket' (use the lower-basket / raise-basket intents). It cannot be picked up or carried; nor can it be opened or closed (it has no lid). Treat it as a fixed feature whose only state is up vs. down. The chain spans both ends of the shaft, so the basket is perceivable from BOTH shaft-room and lower-shaft, but with different prose: from above you see it (or its absence), from below you see it overhead (or at your feet).

items.name: huge diamond

passages.name: oak door
passages.description: A heavy oak slab crossed with iron bands, sealed from within with thick boards. There is no handle or latch on this side.
passages.narratorNote: This is the oak door on the west wall of the living room — boarded shut at game start. After the cyclops smashes east through cyclops-room (magic-flag set), the door is left with a ragged cyclops-shaped hole and becomes traversable both ways. The variants and traversableWhen handle that flip; you just describe what the passage's current description says. Do not call it the 'front door' — there is no separately-modeled outside front door in this story.

variants.text: A heavy oak slab crossed with iron bands — boards still nailed across it, but a ragged cyclops-shaped hole has been smashed clean through the timber, wide enough for a person to step through.

passages.traverseBlockedMessage: The oak door is nailed shut from within.

passages.name: narrow crack
passages.description: A jagged crack in the rock — barely wider than a person's shoulders, narrowing further as it bends out of sight.
passages.narratorNote: Describe the crack atmospherically as a feature of the rock — its narrowness is the canonical observation. ABSOLUTE PROHIBITIONS, no exceptions: (1) NEVER mention any inventory item by name in the same paragraph as the crack. (2) NEVER state that the player's current state, gear, load, or any specific possession is the reason for any obstacle related to the crack — even as a 'factual' aside. 'Too tight while carrying X' is forbidden; so is 'you wouldn't fit with all that gear', or 'if you weren't holding Y...'. (3) NEVER preemptively narrate the crack's traversal status when the player has not attempted to traverse it — if they walked through the room without trying to go through the crack, just mention the crack's existence and physical description. (4) NEVER speculate about future scenarios, hypothetical conditions, or what would let the player pass. The traverseBlockedMessage handles refusal narration when traversal is actually attempted; outside that one moment, the description alone does the work.
passages.traverseBlockedMessage: The crack pinches in tight against you. You can't fit through.

Player says, shouts, whispers, or invokes the word 'temple' or 'treasure' on its own — the magic words tied to the granite walls. Use when the player utters either word as an incantation, not when they ask to walk to a place.

Player waves, raises, brandishes, points, or otherwise invokes the sceptre at the rainbow.
Player winds, twists, turns the key on, cranks, or activates the clockwork canary.
Player brushes, polishes, scrubs, or cleans their teeth using the rubbery putty/glue/gunk.
Player leaps, jumps, dives, or hurls themselves down/off the current location (a tree, a canyon edge, a cliff).

preconditions.failedNarration: There's nothing here you could call rubble.

handler.successNarration: You strain at the splintered timbers, but they're heavy and tightly wedged. Past them the passage narrows to a crack barely a person's width — clearing the rubble wouldn't widen it.

customTools.description: Player jumps into, swims in, dives into, or wades through the Frigid River — without using the boat.
Player rubs, touches, presses, leans against, steps into, or otherwise interacts physically with the mirror.
Player rings, tolls, peals, sounds, or otherwise activates the bell.
Read the contents of an item with readable text — book pages, leaflet, prayer inscription, matchbook cover, etc. Pass the id of an item from the current view's itemsHere or inventory.

itemId.description: id of the item to read

customTools.description: Player flips through, browses, skims, or reads pages other than the dog-eared marked page (569) of the black book. NOT for reading the prayer itself (that's the regular `read` action on the book).

preconditions.failedNarration: You don't have the black book to flip through.

handler.successNarration: You leaf through the heavy black book, past the dog-eared page 569 to other passages. Among the dense archaic script, one line stands out: "In the banishing of evil, certain noises, lights, and prayers prove most effective."

customTools.description: Player plugs, repairs, patches, or applies putty to the punctured boat.
Player launches, shoves off, casts off, releases, or pays out the boat into the river.

then.message:
**** You are dead ****

The Land of the Living Dead opens to receive you. As you cross its threshold, the air thickens with the murmur of those who came before — adventurers, fools, the unlucky. You take your place among them.

else.text: From a great distance, an unseen voice intones: "Adventurer, you have not yet earned the deepest dark." A hand you cannot see takes you up.

You tie the rope securely to the railing. It hangs down into the room below.
As you take the rope, it slips free of the railing.
You hold out the egg. The songbird hops to it and sings a single perfect trill — and the delicate clasp springs open, revealing a golden clockwork canary nestled inside.
You drag the rug aside. Beneath it, set flush into the floor, is a closed wooden trap door.

The cyclops's eye widens in stark terror. He bellows, scrambles backward, and crashes east through the wall — leaving a cyclops-shaped hole that opens straight into the living room. The way up the staircase is now unguarded.
The word leaves your lips and the granite wall seems to drink it in. A grey lurch swallows the room — and when it lets go, the wall stands behind you, on its far side.
You speak the word; the granite wall yawns into grey nothing, the world heaves once, and sets you down beyond it.
The cyclops snatches the sandwich and devours it in two bites. He grunts and licks his lips.
The cyclops drains the bottle in one long pull, water running down his beard.
Sated and slaked, the cyclops slumps against the wall and begins snoring thunderously. The staircase is no longer guarded.
Thank you very much. It really hit the spot.
Thank you very much. You were rather thirsty.
The water spills out and vanishes into the ground.
You kneel before the altar. A gentle warmth gathers around you, and the temple fades. When your senses clear, you are standing in a sunlit forest.
The troll lies helpless. Your weapon descends and ends him with a single, brutal blow.

then.text: The axe whirls end-over-end and buries itself in the troll's chest. He topples.
The axe slams into the troll's shoulder, drawing blood. He pulls it free and drops it.

else.text: The axe sails wide and clatters against the wall.

then.text: Your rusty knife scratches the troll.
You stab the troll deep — the knife's tip snaps off.

else.text: The troll sneers and bats your knife aside.

then.text: Your blade bites the troll for a light wound; greenish blood wells.
Your blade carves a serious gash across the troll's chest.
A killing blow! The troll roars and topples in a pool of greenish blood.
Your blade strikes the troll's temple — he crumples, unconscious.
Your blade sweeps the troll's leg — he goes down hard.
Your blade cracks the troll's axe-grip — the bloody axe spins to the floor.
Your weapon sticks fast in the troll's hide — you can't pull it free!

else.text: The troll dodges your swing.

then.text: The troll's axe nicks you.
The troll's axe bites deep!
The troll's axe strikes a mortal blow!

else.text: The troll swings wildly and misses.

triggers.narration: The troll heaves himself back to his feet.

then.text: You wrench the weapon free.

else.text: You pull, but the weapon stays fast.

triggers.narration: Your sword finally works free with a wet jerk.
Your axe finally works free with a wet jerk.
The troll collapses in a pool of greenish blood. His bloody axe falls at his feet. The east and west passages out of the room are no longer guarded.

effects.value: Your breath rattles, and you slump beside the pile of bones. You have joined its inhabitants.

then.value: Your wounds overcome you, and the world fades to black.

triggers.narration: From above, an unseen hand slams the trap door shut. You hear the click of a latch.
The brass lantern flickers on, casting a warm glow.
The lantern's battery is dead. Nothing happens.
The lantern goes dark.

then.text: Your lamp dims, flickers, and goes dark.
Your candles have burned down to nothing — the flames sputter and die.
A sudden gust of wind sweeps through the cave and snuffs your candles out.

effects.value: Oh, no! You have walked into the slavering fangs of a lurking grue!

triggers.narration: The troll bares his yellow tusks and growls.
Your prattling has worn through the last shred of the troll's patience. 'TROLL SMASH!' he bellows, and lunges at you.
The cyclops eyes you with growing impatience.
The cyclops sniffs the air greedily and takes a step toward you.
The cyclops's patience snaps. 'I HUNGER!' he bellows, and his great hand swings down at you!

then.text: The cyclops cuffs you with the back of his hand — your vision swims.

then.value: The cyclops's hand catches you across the temple. Your vision swims, then fades.

then.text: The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall!

then.value: The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall — and you do not get up.

then.text: The cyclops grabs you in a crushing grip — you feel ribs crack!

then.value: The cyclops squeezes. Ribs crack. Then the rest of you.

then.text: The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. Stars bloom behind your eyes.

then.value: The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. The stars behind your eyes go out.

else.text: The cyclops swings — too slow. You dance aside.

then.text: Your blade glances off the cyclops's hide — a shallow scratch. He laughs.
Your blade finds a gap in the cyclops's hide and bites deep!

else.text: You swing — the cyclops doesn't even flinch. Your blade rings off his skin.

triggers.narration: The cyclops's massive bulk shudders, then crashes to the floor with a sound like a falling tree. He does not move again. The staircase is no longer guarded.
Somewhere in the darkness, you hear the soft scuff of a boot on stone. You are no longer alone in the underground.

then.text: A black-gloved hand snakes toward your pocket — you slap it away.
'A trifle, surely?' the thief murmurs, and the egg vanishes from your possession.
'How droll.' The bag of coins slips from your grasp into the thief's cloak.
The chalice is plucked from in front of you with a flick of the thief's wrist.
The painting — gone. The thief inclines his head in mock apology.
The jade figurine disappears into the folds of the thief's cloak.
The scarab — gone in a heartbeat. The thief tips an imaginary hat.
'A pleasure.' The bracelet is plucked from your wrist (or the floor) into the thief's palm.
Your blade lands — a glancing wound. The thief hisses.
Your blade bites deep. The thief reels.
It's curtains for the thief as your nasty knife removes his head.

else.text: The thief evades your blade.

triggers.narration: Almost as soon as the thief breathes his last breath, a cloud of sinister black fog envelops him, and when the fog lifts, the carcass has disappeared. As the thief dies, the power of his magic decreases, and his treasures reappear in the Treasure Room.
The thief draws his stiletto with a sigh of regret — 'A duel, then. How... regrettable.'

then.text: The thief lunges; you barely evade. The stiletto comes within a hair of you.
The stiletto bites home; the wound is shallow but it bleeds freely.
The blade slips past your guard and drives deep. You stagger.

else.text: A flick of the wrist, faster than you can follow — the stiletto plunges deep.

effects.value: The thief, a man of culture, regrets the unpleasantness — but a duel is a duel. He wipes the stiletto on his cloak and is gone.

effects.text: Your breath catches; the wounds are starting to tell.
Blood slicks your hands and slows your grip.
The world narrows. Each step costs more than the last.

then.text: You are in perfect health.
You have a light wound — scrapes and bruises, nothing more. It will mend on its own as you rest. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)
You have a serious wound. Your strength will return if you rest and keep out of danger. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)
You have serious wounds and should not press your luck. Rest, away from danger, and they will slowly mend. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)

else.text: You are barely alive. One more solid blow would surely finish you. (Health: {flag.player-health} of 30.)

then.text: You have been killed before; the Great Underground Empire does not grant many second chances.

effects.text: The troll's roars grow ragged; he favors one leg.
The troll snarls, blood matting his fur; his axe arm trembles.
The troll sways, axe drooping; one more blow looks likely to finish him.
The cyclops bellows in pain, his single eye wild.
The cyclops's breath comes in wet rasps; blood streaks his chest.
The cyclops staggers, breath labored, the bloodlust dimming.
The thief's elegant guard frays; sweat darkens his cloak.
The thief's smile is gone; he fights now with grim economy.
The thief's stiletto wavers; his composure cracks at last.

triggers.narration: The thief looks you over with cool calculation, weighing what you carry. He does not attack — yet.

You hear a scream of anguish as you violate the robber's hideaway. Using passages unknown to you, he rushes to its defense. The thief gestures mysteriously, and the treasures in the room suddenly vanish.
The thief takes the egg between two long fingers, examines it, and gives a small approving smile. With an audible click the clasp yields. He pockets the egg, canary and all. 'A pleasure, as always.'
The thief accepts your offering, turns it over in his long fingers with a connoisseur's eye, and tucks it away in his great bag with a small, satisfied nod.
There is no reaction.
The skeleton key fits the skull-and-crossbones lock with a satisfying click; the grate's bolt slides free.
You turn the skeleton key in the skull-and-crossbones lock; the bolt slides home.
The grate is locked closed — a skull-and-crossbones lock keeps it shut.
The key doesn't fit.
The key doesn't fit.
You untie the rope from the railing and coil it back into your hand.
There's nothing to tie that to here.
There's nothing here to untie.
There is no reaction.
It won't catch fire.
Nothing happens.
Nothing happens.
There's nothing to lower here.
There's nothing to raise here.
It doesn't turn.
There's nothing to inflate here.
Nothing to plug.
Nothing to dig here.
It is already alight.
There is nothing here to put out.

then.text: The rug already lies in a heap to one side.

else.text: There is no rug here to move.

then.text: Your word rings out, but the cyclops is already long gone.

else.text: You utter the word, but nothing here stirs to it.

triggers.narration: You speak the word aloud. Nothing answers.
You bow your head, but this is no place of worship.
Nothing of yours is stuck fast.

then.text: Your voice carries clear through the quiet room.

else.text: Your voice goes out and simply fades.

then.text: You raise the sceptre and wave it, but nothing here answers the gesture.

else.text: You have no sceptre to wave.

triggers.narration: You have nothing like that to wind.
You have nothing to brush them with.
There is nowhere here to leap down to.
You have nothing on you to do that with.
There is no water here to swim in.
There is no mirror here to rub.

then.text: You have the putty ready, but nothing here is torn or leaking.

else.text: You have nothing to patch anything with.

triggers.narration: There is a sickening crunching noise. The egg's clasp gives way, and the bird inside is silenced under the wreckage.
Your indelicate handling bends the egg's clasp out of true and buckles the gold inlay. The clockwork bird inside is mangled — silver beak crumpled, ruby eyes scratched, fine wires kinked beyond use.
A cool draft seems to flow up from the floor of the temple — a passage somewhere has opened.
As you take the coffin in hand, the draft from below ceases. The way down has sealed itself again.

Your voice goes out — and instead of the cacophony you've been suffering, perfect silence answers. The acoustics of the room have changed. A platinum bar gleams on the floor where the noise had hidden it.

You raise the sceptre and trace a slow figure in the air. The rainbow shudders and solidifies — its colors hardening into a luminous bridge of gold and prismatic light. At your feet, a heavy pot of gold appears, gleaming where the rainbow now touches earth.

An almost inaudible voice whispers in your ear: 'Look to your treasures for the final secret.' Across the room, the trophy case glitters; in the depths of the wood, an ancient map appears — and far above ground, the way to a stone barrow stands open.

effects.message:
**** You have completed the Great Underground Empire ****

A voice calls out from the depths of the barrow: 'Welcome, adventurer. Your final score: {flag.score} (out of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This earns you the rank of {rank}. Your name shall be inscribed in the chronicles of those who have braved the Empire and prevailed.'

The stone slab settles back into place behind you. Your adventure is at an end.

triggers.narration: You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.
You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.

then.text: Fweep! The bat seizes you by the scruff of the neck and lifts you away through utter blackness. You are deposited, dazed, in a coal mine.
Fweep! The bat carries you through tunnels you cannot see, depositing you in another part of the mine.
Fweep! The bat hauls you off into the darkness and drops you somewhere deep underground.
Fweep! The bat seizes you and bears you off to a distant shaft.
Fweep! The bat carries you up a narrow shaft and drops you at the top.
Fweep! The bat plummets with you down a long, dark drop and releases you at the bottom.
Fweep! The bat takes you on a winding journey and drops you in a squeaky-floored cavern.

else.text: Fweep! The bat carries you back near where the mining began.

then.text: In your confusion, the candles drop to the ground (and they are out).

The bell suddenly becomes red hot and falls to the ground. The wraiths, as if paralyzed, stop their jeering and slowly turn to face you. On their ashen faces, the expression of a long-forgotten terror takes shape.

else.text: The bell tolls — a deep, hollow note that hangs in the air for a long moment, then fades.
There's no bell in your hand.

triggers.narration: Each word of the prayer reverberates through the hall in a deafening confusion. As the last word fades, a voice, loud and commanding, speaks: "Begone, fiends!" A heart-stopping scream fills the cavern, and the spirits, sensing a greater power, flee through the walls.
One of the matches starts to burn.
The matchbook is empty — you've burned all your matches.
You touch the burning match to the candles, lighting them.
You have nothing burning to light them with.
The candles are spent — only stubs of wax remain. There's nothing left to light.
You blow out the candles.
The flames cling to the torch — it can't be extinguished.
The flames flicker wildly and appear to dance. The earth beneath your feet trembles, and your legs nearly buckle beneath you. The spirits cower at your unearthly power.

then.text: The match has gone out.

triggers.narration: You read the prayer aloud. The words have no power; the spirits jeer.
You read the prayer aloud. The words fade in the dim light, leaving no impression.

The machine comes to life with a dazzling display of colored lights and bizarre noises — clattering, whirring, sparks. After a few moments the racket subsides. The lid pops open. Where you placed the coal, a magnificent diamond now lies.
The machine clatters and whirs, but produces only a faint puff of greenish smoke. Whatever it's looking for, it isn't finding.
The machine clatters into life. A plume of green smoke curls up out of the chamber, drifts into the room, and dissipates. The noise dwindles to silence.
The switch doesn't budge.
The switch is too small to manipulate with human fingers — barely 1/16 by 1/4 of an inch.

effects.value: lowered

triggers.narration: The chain rattles as the basket descends out of sight, settling at the bottom of the shaft.

effects.value: raised

triggers.narration: The chain creaks as you haul; the basket rises smoothly out of the depths and settles at the top of the shaft.

then.text: Far above you at the top of the shaft, the wicker basket sways gently on its chain — well out of reach.
The wicker basket sits at your feet at the bottom of the shaft, the chain rising into darkness above.
The basket has been lowered out of sight; the chain disappears down into the shaft.

triggers.narration: Click.
Click.

then.text: Click. The maintenance-room lights flicker out. After a moment they flicker back on — a self-resetting safety circuit, evidently.

else.text: Click. The maintenance-room lights flicker.

triggers.narration: There is a rumbling sound and a stream of water appears to burst from the east wall of the room (apparently, a leak has occurred in a pipe). The water level begins to rise.

effects.value: The water reaches the ceiling and drowns you in the small space. You should have plugged that leak.

triggers.narration: You press the viscous material against the pipe; it spreads and seals the leak with a satisfying squelch. The flow stops. The water level slowly recedes.
Water sprays past your fingers, the pressure forcing your hand right back.
The bolt turns slowly. Somewhere below, the sluice gates open and water pours through the dam with a sustained roar. The reservoir will take some time to drain.
The bolt turns smoothly under the wrench. Below, the sluice gates rumble shut, and water begins to back up behind the dam once again.
The bolt doesn't budge — you'll need the right tool, in the right place, with the right control surface enabled.
The bolt is heavy iron and held tight in its socket. Your bare hands can't budge it.

then.text: With your hands free, you think you might just be able to squeeze through the narrow passage to the west.
With your hands free, you think you might just be able to squeeze back through the narrow passage.

triggers.narration: Your load is too great. The passage west narrows to a slit barely a person's width.

effects.text: You won't be able to get back up to the tunnel you are going through when it gets to the next room.

triggers.narration: Your load is too great. The passage back narrows to a slit barely a person's width.

effects.value: inflated

triggers.narration: The boat hisses and swells into a sturdy yellow raft, ready for the river.
That isn't going to inflate anything.
You blow until your head spins. The folded plastic doesn't budge.

effects.value: deflated

triggers.narration: You smear the putty over the gash. With a final hiss the rip seals; the boat is patched, deflated, and ready to inflate again. The tube of putty is now empty.
You shove off. The current catches the boat and carries you out onto the Frigid River.
You push off the sand. The current takes hold and the boat slips back into the river.
You shove off the narrow strip of beach. The river takes the boat downstream.

then.text: The boat won't set off from here — there's no current to carry it away.

else.text: You push at the magic boat, but it only rocks where it sits. Shoving it from outside accomplishes nothing — a boat carries you downstream only when you are aboard it.

effects.value: punctured

then.value: Something sharp pierces the boat. The plastic shrieks and the river takes you — icy, fast, irresistible. You are pulled under and do not surface.

else.text: Something sharp slips — the boat hisses, sputters, and collapses to a useless pile of plastic. You're spat out onto the riverbank at the foot of the dam.

triggers.narration: The current carries you downstream.
The current carries you downstream, around a bend.
The current carries you faster — the rumble ahead grows louder.
The current sweeps you forward. The roar of falling water fills your ears.

effects.value: Unfortunately, the magic boat doesn't provide any protection from the rocks and boulders one meets at the bottom of waterfalls. Including this one. Aragain Falls roars its indifference.
Oh dear. You seem to have ignited the coal gas. It's great for blowing up dimwitted adventurers who wander into a coal mine with an open flame.

triggers.narration: The egg hits the path with a sharp crack. The shell splits apart, exposing the broken clockwork canary tangled in the wreckage.
It falls through the branches to the forest path below.

The canary chirps, slightly off-key, an aria from a forgotten opera. From out of the greenery flies a lovely songbird. It perches on a limb just over your head and opens its beak to sing. As it does so a beautiful brass bauble drops from its mouth, bounces off the top of your head, and lands glimmering in the grass. As the canary winds down, the songbird flies away.

then.text: There is an unpleasant grinding noise from inside the canary.
The canary chirps blithely, but the songbird does not return.

else.text: The canary chirps, but its song carries little weight here.

triggers.narration: (You note the brass bauble's worth — +1 point. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
The brass bauble joins the trophy case's collection. (+1 point; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The brass bauble leaves the trophy case. -1 point; your score is now {flag.score}.)

effects.value: Well, you seem to have been brushing your teeth with some sort of glue. As a result, your mouth gets glued together (with your nose), and you die of respiratory failure.
The leaves burn, and so do you.

As the knife approaches its victim, your mind is submerged by an overmastering will. Slowly, your hand turns, until the rusty blade is an inch from your neck. The knife seems to sing as it savagely slits your throat.
It's a long way down. You hit hard.
It's a shame the leap you just attempted has done you in.
At the moment the flame touches the book, a guardian materializes from the shadows. With a single, terrible gesture he reduces you to a fine ash.
A guardian materializes from the gloom. He shakes his head sadly, then places your own head on a sharp pole at the entrance to the temple.
If you insist...Poof, you're dead!
The momentum of your throw carries you backward; you crack your skull on the ground. Quite an achievement.

triggers.narration: You dig deeper into the loose sand. The hole grows.

effects.value: The walls of the hole collapse around you, smothering you in cold, wet sand.

triggers.narration:
(with the shovel)
You seem to be digging a hole here.

(with the shovel)
The hole is getting deeper, but that's about it.

(with the shovel)
You are surrounded by a wall of sand on all sides.

(with the shovel)
You can see a scarab here in the sand.

(with the shovel)
The hole is as deep as it'll go without bringing the cave down on your head.
That isn't going to dig much sand.
Digging with your bare hands won't get you very far.
You shred the canvas into ribbons. Whatever it depicted, it depicts no longer — the painting is now worthless.

effects.value: The sceptre snaps in your hands. With it, the rainbow that bridged the falls fades and dissolves; you hang for a moment in empty mist before plummeting to the rocks below.
The Frigid River lives up to its name. The currents catch you and drag you under within seconds; you drown before you can struggle to the surface.

then.text: Your sword is glowing very brightly.
Your sword is glowing with a faint blue glow.
The roar is unbearable — your head is already pounding. You won't last another moment in here unless you can silence the noise.

else.text: It is unbearably loud here, with an ear-splitting roar seeming to come from all around you. There is a pounding in your head which won't stop. With a tremendous effort, you scramble out of the room.

sharedVariants.text: It is pitch black. You are likely to be eaten by a grue.
